standards.Description: Hospital establishes and maintains a comprehensive emergency management program.
Hospital reduces the risk of health care-associated infections.
Hospital uses clinical practice guidelines to design or improve processes.
Hospital safely manages high-alert and hazardous medications.
Use at least two patient identifiers when providing care.
Reduce likelihood of patient harm associated with anticoagulant therapy.
Hospital assesses and manages patient's pain.
Hospital maintains complete and accurate medical records.
Hospital respects, protects, and promotes patient rights.
OPPE data is used to identify professional practice trends.
Building and fire protection features maintained.
Hospital assesses staff competency.
Hospital must have a QAPI program that measures and tracks quality indicators.
Hospital must have an effective discharge planning process.
Patients have the right to make informed decisions about their care.

standards.Standard Title: Patient Rights - Care in Safe Setting
standards.Description: Patient has right to receive care in a safe setting.
Hospital must collect data on performance measures.
Hospital must have pharmaceutical services meeting patient needs.
Hospital must have organized food and dietetic services.
Hospital must maintain an active infection prevention and control program.
Exposure control plan for bloodborne pathogens.
Respiratory protection program requirements.
Written hazard communication program and SDS availability.
Fire extinguisher placement, maintenance, and employee training.
OSHA 300 log maintenance and injury reporting requirements.
Minimum nurse staffing ratios and qualification requirements.
Hospital physical environment maintenance and safety requirements.
24/7 emergency services availability and capabilities.
Operating room standards and surgical safety requirements.
Clinical laboratory standards and CLIA compliance.

surveys.Notes: Three-year accreditation survey. Two condition-level findings identified in EC and IC chapters.
CMS validation survey following Joint Commission accreditation. Minor deficiencies in discharge planning.
Internal mock survey to prepare for upcoming state inspection. Critical finding in medication management.
Annual state health department inspection. Awaiting official report.
Pre-triennial mock survey with external consulting firm.
Focused review on ED throughput and patient boarding. Minor observations noted.

findings.Description: Emergency management plan not updated to reflect current hazard vulnerability analysis. Annual review documentation missing for 2 consecutive years.
findings.Evidence Required: Updated HVA, revised EM plan, training documentation

findings.Description: Hand hygiene compliance rates below threshold in ICU. Monitoring data shows 68% compliance vs 90% target.
findings.Surveyor Notes: Systemic issue requiring immediate corrective action.
findings.Evidence Required: Hand hygiene monitoring data, education records, improvement plan

findings.Description: High-alert medication storage not consistently segregated in medication rooms on 3 North and 4 West.
findings.Evidence Required: Photos of corrected storage, policy update, staff education

findings.Description: Operative reports not consistently completed within 24 hours. 15% of sampled records exceeded timeframe.
findings.Evidence Required: Documentation audit results, corrective action plan

findings.Description: Observed one instance of blood draw without two-identifier verification.
Discharge planning not initiated within 24 hours of admission for 20% of ICU patients sampled.
QAPI performance measures for laboratory turnaround times not consistently tracked for 3 months.
Informed consent forms missing interpreter attestation for 2 LEP patients.
Waiting room seating arrangement does not support respiratory hygiene station visibility.
Medication dispensing cabinet temperature log gap of 48 hours noted on one unit.
Oxytocin infusion protocol deviations noted in 3 of 10 sampled charts. Dosing increments exceeded policy limits.
findings.Surveyor Notes: High-risk medication concern requiring immediate action.
findings.Evidence Required: Protocol compliance audit, education records, process change

findings.Description: Two fire doors on 2nd floor found with door hold-open devices not connected to fire alarm system.
Annual competency assessments overdue for 3 radiology technologists.
INR monitoring documentation incomplete for 1 patient on warfarin therapy.
Nurse staffing ratio fell below 1:2 in ICU during 3 shifts in the past month. Documentation of variance reporting incomplete.
findings.Surveyor Notes: Patient safety concern. Corrective action plan required within 10 days.
findings.Evidence Required: Staffing plan, variance reports, contingency staffing protocol

findings.Description: Emergency generator load testing documentation incomplete. Monthly records missing for 2 consecutive months.
findings.Surveyor Notes: Must demonstrate compliance before next inspection.

findings.Description: Emergency department triage protocol documentation not updated to reflect current ESI v4 guidelines.
Proficiency testing records filing system inconsistent between main lab and satellite locations.
Pain reassessment documentation after medication administration not consistently completed in ED.
Patient rights information brochure not available in Somali language in ED waiting area.

corrective-actions.Description: Complete comprehensive update of emergency management plan including new HVA, updated contact lists, and revised evacuation procedures.
corrective-actions.Root Cause: Turnover in facilities management leadership led to missed annual review cycle.

corrective-actions.Finding Description: Hand hygiene compliance below threshold in ICU
corrective-actions.Description: Implement enhanced hand hygiene monitoring program with real-time feedback, monthly compliance reports, and peer coaching program.
corrective-actions.Root Cause: Lack of real-time feedback mechanism and inconsistent monitoring methodology.

corrective-actions.Description: Redesign medication room storage with color-coded bins, warning labels, and physical separation for high-alert medications.
corrective-actions.Root Cause: Medication room renovation completed without pharmacy safety review.
corrective-actions.Verification Method: Physical inspection of all medication rooms

corrective-actions.Description: Implement automated discharge planning trigger in EHR at time of ICU admission. Add case manager rounding to daily ICU workflow.
corrective-actions.Root Cause: No systematic trigger for discharge planning initiation in ICU. Process dependent on individual case manager awareness.
corrective-actions.Verification Method: Chart audit of 30 consecutive ICU admissions

corrective-actions.Description: Revise oxytocin administration protocol with hard stops in infusion pump library. Implement mandatory double-check process.
corrective-actions.Root Cause: Infusion pump library did not enforce maximum dosing increments. Protocol language was ambiguous.
corrective-actions.Verification Method: Pump library validation and 30-day compliance audit

corrective-actions.Finding Description: Fire doors not connected to alarm system
corrective-actions.Description: Reconnect fire door hold-open devices to fire alarm system. Test all fire doors on affected floor. Schedule facility-wide fire door audit.
corrective-actions.Root Cause: Door hold-open devices disconnected during renovation and not reconnected at project close-out.

corrective-actions.Finding Description: ICU nurse staffing ratio below standard
corrective-actions.Description: Develop contingency staffing plan with float pool, agency agreements, and mandatory overtime protocols. Implement daily staffing projection tool.
corrective-actions.Root Cause: Insufficient float pool staffing combined with unexpected census surge. No formal contingency plan.

corrective-actions.Description: Implement electronic generator testing log with automated reminders. Train all facilities staff on documentation requirements.
corrective-actions.Root Cause: Paper-based logging system with no reminder mechanism. Staff turnover in maintenance team.
corrective-actions.Verification Method: Documentation audit of 3 consecutive monthly tests

corrective-actions.Description: Add 24-hour operative report completion reminder in EHR. Include metric in surgeon performance dashboard.
corrective-actions.Root Cause: No automated reminder system. Surgeons unaware of compliance rate.

corrective-actions.Description: Complete overdue competency assessments. Implement automated tracking system with 60-day advance notifications.
corrective-actions.Root Cause: Manual tracking spreadsheet not maintained during department reorganization.

corrective-actions.Description: Update triage protocol to ESI v4 guidelines. Conduct staff education and competency validation.
corrective-actions.Root Cause: Protocol revision oversight during annual policy review cycle.
corrective-actions.Verification Method: Policy committee approval and staff sign-off

corrective-actions.Finding Description: Pain reassessment documentation gaps in ED
corrective-actions.Description: Create standardized pain reassessment documentation template in EHR with automated timing reminders after medication administration.
corrective-actions.Root Cause: No EHR prompt for pain reassessment. Documentation relied on individual nurse recall.
corrective-actions.Verification Method: Chart audit of 50 consecutive ED encounters

corrective-actions.Description: Revise informed consent form to include mandatory interpreter attestation field. Update surgical pre-op checklist.
corrective-actions.Root Cause: Consent form template did not include interpreter attestation section.
corrective-actions.Verification Method: Review of 20 LEP patient consent forms

corrective-actions.Description: Implement automated QAPI dashboard for laboratory turnaround time tracking with monthly reporting.
corrective-actions.Root Cause: Manual data collection process was unsustainable. Staff resource constraints.
corrective-actions.Verification Method: 3 consecutive months of complete QAPI data

corrective-actions.Finding Description: Patient rights brochure not in Somali
corrective-actions.Description: Translate patient rights brochure into Somali. Audit all public-facing materials for language access compliance.
corrective-actions.Root Cause: Language needs assessment not updated to reflect demographic changes in service area.
corrective-actions.Verification Method: Physical audit of all patient-facing materials

policies.Content Summary: Comprehensive infection prevention program including surveillance, outbreak management, hand hygiene, and isolation protocols.
Hospital-wide emergency preparedness, response, and recovery plan. Includes HVA, communications, and resource management.
Storage, handling, prescribing, and administration protocols for high-alert medications including insulin, heparin, and opioids.
Two-identifier verification process for all patient care activities including medication administration, blood draws, and procedures.
OSHA-compliant exposure control plan including universal precautions, sharps safety, PPE requirements, and post-exposure protocols.
Process for obtaining informed consent including interpreter requirements, risk disclosure, and documentation standards.
Timely discharge planning initiation, patient/family education, follow-up scheduling, and transition of care coordination.
N95 fit testing, PAPR use, medical evaluations, and training requirements for respiratory protection.
Fire response procedures, RACE/PASS, fire drill requirements, and life safety code compliance.
PHI handling, access controls, breach notification, and minimum necessary standards.
Annual competency assessment framework including skills validation, continuing education, and remediation.
GHS-compliant hazard communication including SDS management, labeling, and employee training.

evidence.Document Name: ICU Hand Hygiene Compliance Data Q1-Q3
evidence.Description: Monthly hand hygiene compliance monitoring data showing improvement from 68% to 94%.
Before/after photos of high-alert medication storage areas on 3 North and 4 West.
Monthly audit results showing operative report completion within 24 hours improved to 98%.
Staff training completion report for annual bloodborne pathogen training - 99% completion.
Screenshots of automated QAPI dashboard showing 3 months of complete turnaround time data.
Updated informed consent form template with mandatory interpreter attestation field.
Fire drill completion report with response times and staff participation rates.
Annual N95 fit testing completion records for all clinical staff.

rounds.Area Inspected: ED Main, Trauma Bay, Fast Track
rounds.Notes: Minor issues: expired eyewash station, blocked electrical panel, hand sanitizer dispenser empty.
Critical: Fire door hold-open device issue on 2nd floor (linked to FND-012). Minor: Exit sign bulb out on 3rd floor.

rounds.Area Inspected: ICU Pods A-D, Clean Utility, Soiled Utility
rounds.Notes: Significant improvement in hand hygiene compliance. Minor: one alcohol hand rub dispenser not mounted at correct height.
Excellent compliance with surgical safety checklist, timeout process, and patient identification. No findings.

rounds.Area Inspected: Central Pharmacy, Satellite Pharmacies, Medication Rooms
rounds.Notes: Medication management system tracer focusing on high-alert medications and look-alike/sound-alike drugs.
IC program tracer in preparation for mock survey. Focus on hand hygiene, isolation, and environmental cleaning.

Evidence Uploaded for EVD-001 in ICU
Finding Created for FND-015 in ICU

CAPA Verified for CAPA-002 in ICU
Finding Closed for FND-003 in Pharmacy
Department Score Updated for DEPT-002 in ICU
CAPA Closed for CAPA-003 in Pharmacy
Finding Status Changed for FND-006 in ICU
Policy Approved for POL-009 in Facilities Management
